title: Menu sub-screens need FF6-style bordered panels and proper layout
description:
Menu sub-screens (Item, Equipment, Magic, Abilities, Formation, Config, Status) display content directly on the main panel background without their own bordered containers. This makes them look unpolished and hard to use.

Issues observed:
1. Sub-screen content overlaps with party member rows in MainPanel
2. No distinct bordered panel per sub-screen (FF6 uses separate windowed panels)
3. Config settings list extends beyond visible area
4. Formation list overlaps with party display

Reference: FF6 menu design uses distinct bordered panels for each sub-screen:
- Items: tabbed header (USE/ARRANGE/RARE) + description + item list in bordered box
- Config: setting name + value pairs in bordered box with description footer
- Equipment: slot list + stat comparison in bordered boxes
- Magic: character info + spell grid in bordered box

Fix: Each sub-screen should have its own PanelContainer with the game's border style. When a sub-screen is active, it should fully replace the MainPanel content area (or display as a new bordered panel). Content should be contained within the panel using VBox/HBox containers.

This is a dedicated UI polish pass affecting all 9 menu sub-screen scenes and their associated scripts.
close_reason: All 7 menu sub-screens restructured with FF6-style bordered PanelContainers. Full viewport replacement mode. Gap 3.4 Phase 3 COMPLETE.

close_reason: Viewport changed to 1280x720 with 4x camera zoom. All UI overlays scaled.

title: UI text overlap issues across multiple screens at 320x180
description:
Multiple UI screens have text overlap and layout problems at the native 320x180 resolution. Found during manual testing of PR #130 Wilds Route.

Issues observed:
1. **Battle party panel**: Member names, HP/MP values, and status all overlap on a single line. Labels are positioned absolutely without accounting for text width.
2. **Dialogue box**: Speaker name (VESSA) overlaps with dialogue text. The name label and text label share the same vertical space.
3. **Save point menu**: Rest and Rest & Save menu items overlap. Menu labels are positioned too close vertically.
4. **Dialogue scrolling**: Cannot advance dialogue with keyboard/Enter — requires mouse click. Should respond to ui_accept action.

Root cause: All UI scenes use absolute pixel positioning designed for a larger viewport. At 320x180, the default font size creates text that is too large relative to the layout containers. The scenes need proper VBoxContainer/MarginContainer layouts that scale with content, not hardcoded pixel offsets.

Affects: battle_ui.gd, battle_party_panel.gd, dialogue_box.gd, save_load.gd, menu screens
Related: ui-design.md specifies layouts but scenes were built with approximate positioning
close_reason: Resolved by viewport change (1280x720) and container-based layouts in PR #130 and menu UI polish.

description: game/project.godot is missing window/stretch/aspect setting. technical-architecture.md and the tech architecture spec both require aspect=keep. Without it, Godot defaults to ignore which stretches on non-16:9 displays instead of pillarboxing/letterboxing. Invisible on most monitors but distorts on ultrawide or 4:3.

title: Add skip input handler to cutscene_player.gd
description: cutscene_player.gd has no _unhandled_input method. skip_cutscene() exists but is dead code from the player perspective — only programmatic callers or auto-skip via cutscene_seen_* flag use it. Need to add ui_cancel or similar input handler so players can skip cutscenes. Accessibility concern.

title: Clarify Despair stack clearing vs rest item requirement
description: dungeons-world.md:1842 says Despair stacks persist until the next save point rest. Ambiguous whether the free fallback (25% MP only) counts as rest for clearing stacks. Since Pallor Wastes have no shops, players may arrive without rest items. Design decision needed: does free fallback clear Despair stacks?
close_reason: Despair stacks clear at any save point interaction including free fallback

title: Fix Caldera inn price contradiction in economy.md
description: economy.md:146 table says Caldera inn = 300g (450g without Employee Card). But economy.md:153-156 note calculates inflated = 300g, with-card = 225g. The table and note present incompatible numbers. Reconcile using the Caldera shop 150% markup model.
close_reason: Fixed Caldera inn price table to match note math (225g/300g)

title: 5 crafting materials in dungeons-city.md missing from items.md material list
description: dungeons-city.md references 5 crafting materials not in items.md Complete Material List: Salvaged Automaton Core (Corrund:355), Molten Core (Caldera:499, likely should be Molten Gear), Ley Crystal Shard (Catacombs:228, likely should be Ley Crystal Fragment), Emberstone (Ashmark:611), Nest Mothers Core (Caldera:523). Either items.md needs new entries or dungeons-city.md has naming inconsistencies.
close_reason: 2 naming fixes applied (Ley Crystal Shard->Fragment, Molten Core->Gear); 3 genuinely missing materials filed as GitHub Issue #36

title: Ashen Ram gold: 5,000 (act-ii.md) vs 2,250 (bosses.md)
description: The Ashen Ram has Gold=5,000 in bestiary/act-ii.md:107 but Gold=2,250 in bestiary/bosses.md:506. Large discrepancy (2x+). Bosses.md is authoritative.

title: Despair Shard steal item missing from items.md
description: Grey Cleaver Unbound (Stage 2) in act-iii.md:272 has Steal=Despair Shard (100%) but Despair Shard does not exist anywhere in items.md — not in materials, key items, or consumables. Undefined item.

title: The Architect gold: bosses.md (4,000g) vs act-iii.md (5,000g)
description: The Architect Stage 1 has gold=4,000 in bosses.md but gold=5,000 in act-iii.md. Grey Cleaver Unbound Stage 2 has 280g in bosses.md vs 5,000g in act-iii.md. Bestiary is authoritative — act-iii.md needs reconciliation.

title: Arcanite Shard: dungeons-world.md says '50 MP' but should restore AC
description: dungeons-world.md describes Arcanite Shard as restoring 50 MP, but abilities.md and crafting.md say it restores AC. Likely stale from before the AC system was designed. Also: the AC restoration amount (3 AC) is defined in crafting.md but not in abilities.md or items.md — propagate.

title: Reconcile combat-formulas.md Regular Enemy HP table with bestiary
description: combat-formulas.md Section 'Regular Enemy HP by Act' lists Act III enemies at 6,000-14,000 HP, but actual bestiary (act-iii.md) has Act III regulars at 1,000-2,247 HP. The table appears to be aspirational targets written before the bestiary was populated. The bestiary is authoritative. The table needs updating to match actual enemy stats across all acts. Discovered during Gap 3.4 (Difficulty & Balance) PR #33 Copilot review.

title: story-review-loop: expand propagation agent to cover spec/plan duplicates
description: The Propagation agent checks story docs thoroughly but treats spec/plan docs as secondary. When events.md gets a fix in one round, the spec's mirrored sections (e.g., Section 5 duplicates the reload flow from events.md 2c) are not flagged for update. Example: PR #14 round 1 fixed events.md reload step but the spec still said 'Full HP/MP as of last save' -- Copilot caught this in review round 3. Fix: expand the Propagation agent prompt to explicitly check whether spec/plan docs duplicate any text that was just fixed in story docs. When events.md section 2c changes, Sections 4-5 of the spec must be checked for stale duplicates.
close_reason: Implemented in 50eead7 - added full-section re-read rule, spec/plan mirror check, and Pass K hygiene checks to story-review-loop skill

title: story-review-loop: add post-fix section re-read rule
description: When the story-review-loop fixes an issue, it does a propagation sweep (grep entity across files) but does NOT re-read the surrounding section to check if the fix introduced a new self-contradiction with adjacent text. Example: PR #14 round 1 fixed HP/MP reload wording on line 387-389, but the simplifying principle on lines 405-407 now contradicted it. Copilot caught this; our agents did not. Fix: add a rule to the fix step (Section 2c of the skill) requiring the agent to re-read the ENTIRE section after editing, not just ±10 lines, and verify the fix is consistent with every claim in the section.
close_reason: Implemented in 50eead7 - added full-section re-read rule, spec/plan mirror check, and Pass K hygiene checks to story-review-loop skill

title: Clean up old files, update README, create PR
description: Remove old server/ and client/ dirs. Update README. Run full test + typecheck. Create PR. See docs/plans/2026-03-07-typescript-monorepo-refactor.md Tasks 5-6.

title: No test coverage for _do_item consume path and restock_event filtering
description: PR #147 review: test_battle_state.gd has no test for battle_manager._do_item() item consumption. test_shop_overlay.gd has no test for restock_event filtering. Both are pre-existing gaps exposed by the review.

title: Weapons JSON uses mixed types for tier field
description: PR #147 review: weapons.json uses integer tiers for most weapons but string 'forged' for arcanite weapons. Code reading tier must handle both types. Standardize to one type (e.g., int tier + bool is_forged).

title: Ember Tonic references nonexistent Floor 4 in consumables.json
description: PR #147 review: consumables.json ember_tonic has availability 'Ember Vein chest (Floor 4)' but only floors 1-3 exist in game data. Either fix the availability text or add the floor.

title: Add warp_walker_boots and gravity_shard to equipment.md
description: Found during PR #147 review. Two new Dry Well of Aelhart Floor 6 accessories were added to accessories.json but not documented in the canonical equipment.md reference.

title: Remove dead push_save_overlay() from game_manager.gd
description: Found during PR #147 review. game_manager.gd:140-143 push_save_overlay() has zero callers after menu_overlay._open_save() was refactored to use direct pop_overlay(true) + push_overlay(). Should be removed.

title: required_flags tests duplicate logic instead of exercising real handlers
description: test_required_flags.gd tests re-implement the comma-splitting flag-checking logic locally instead of calling the actual handler code paths (_on_dialogue_trigger_entered, on_cutscene_trigger_entered). Can pass even if real handlers regress. Should create Area2D triggers with metadata and exercise real code paths. From Copilot comment on PR #147.

title: Tests access private _-prefixed fields instead of public getters
description: 16+ instances across test files (test_exploration, test_required_flags, test_ember_vein_f3, test_cutscene_player, test_shop_overlay, etc.) access private fields like _player, _current_map, _in_cutscene when public getters exist (get_player(), get_current_map(), is_in_cutscene()). A sweep to update all test files would resolve. Discovered during PR #147 godot-review-loop.

title: menu_overlay.gd save command has one-frame overlay gap
description: menu_overlay.gd:266-273 _open_save() calls deferred push_save_overlay then immediate pop_overlay. One-frame gap where game is unpaused with no overlay. Pending CONNECT_ONE_SHOT handlers fire prematurely, player can move/interact during transition. Found in PR #147 godot-review-loop round 3.

title: Shop JSON schema diverges from technical-architecture.md Section 2.4
description: All 5 Valdris shop JSON files wrap data in 'shop' object and use per-item 'restock_event', 'stock_limit', 'buy_price' fields not in the spec. Spec expects top-level keys and shop-level restock_events array. Either code or spec needs updating. Found in PR #147 godot-review-loop round 1.

title: save-system.md uses camelCase keys (leyCrystals) but code uses snake_case (ley_crystals)
description: docs/story/save-system.md:183 uses camelCase (leyCrystals, arcaniteCharges, deviceLoadout) but save_manager.gd:222 validates snake_case (ley_crystals). Doc/code naming mismatch could cause implementation bugs. Found in PR #147 godot-review-loop round 2.

title: hi_potion available_act:1 in roothollow_herbalist and aelhart_general shops
description: items.md says Hi-Potion is Act II shops. valdris_crown_general was fixed in PR #147 but roothollow_herbalist.json:9 and aelhart_general.json:39 still have available_act:1. May be intentional early access or need design decision. Found in PR #147 godot-review-loop round 2.

title: Missing game data: Ley-Lantern, Pallor Antidote, Warp Walker's Boots, Gravity Shard, Composite Shortbow
description: Design docs reference items/equipment that don't exist in game/data/ yet: Ley-Lantern (city-valdris.md general shop, 60g), Pallor Antidote (city-valdris.md specialty Interlude, 400g), Warp Walker's Boots (dungeons-world.md Floor 6 chest, +20 SPD), Gravity Shard (dungeons-world.md Floor 6 chest, +12 all stats), Composite Shortbow (city-valdris.md weaponsmith, ATK +12 SPD +3, 450g). Found in PR #147 godot-review-loop round 2.

title: Test files incomplete singleton cleanup in before_each
description: 7 test files (test_scene_7_flags, test_scene_7_integration, test_scene_7_ordering, test_anchor_oar_regression, test_lower_ward_regression, test_valdris_integration, test_valdris_shops) don't clear all singletons in before_each per checklist (missing GameManager.transition_data, PartyState, SaveManager). Also test_issue_fixes.gd directly assigns GameManager.current_overlay bypassing lifecycle. Found in PR #147 godot-review-loop rounds 1-2.

title: Encapsulation: exploration_entity_manager connects to private _on_* methods
description: exploration_entity_manager.gd:192-232 connects entity signals directly to _exploration._on_npc_interacted, _on_chest_opened, etc. — 18 private method references. Violates encapsulation rule from PR #135. Should use public methods or signal relay. Found in PR #147 godot-review-loop round 1.

title: royal_signet missing from accessories.json and jeweler shop
description: city-valdris.md Jeweler table lists Royal Signet at 1200g Act II with court favor requirement. It only exists in materials.json as a material drop, not in accessories.json as an equippable. The jeweler shop has no Act II items. Found during PR #146 godot-review-loop round 2.

title: waystone price and location mismatch between general shop and city-valdris.md
description: valdris_crown_general.json sells waystone at 300g in the general shop. city-valdris.md places it in Specialty Goods at 500g Act I. consumables.json buy_price is 300. The canonical spec and JSON disagree on both price and shop location. Found during PR #146 godot-review-loop round 2.

title: Test files missing comprehensive singleton cleanup in before_each/after_each
description: Multiple test files lack complete singleton cleanup. Missing EventFlags.clear_all() in: test_shop_overlay.gd, test_anchor_oar_regression.gd, test_lower_ward_regression.gd, test_scene_7_ordering.gd. Missing PartyState cleanup in: test_scene_7_integration.gd, test_scene_7_flags.gd. Missing DataManager.clear_cache() in: test_scene_7_ordering.gd, test_valdris_integration.gd. Found during PR #146 godot-review-loop round 1.

title: Refactor ExplorationEntityManager to use public accessor instead of private _entities
description: exploration_entity_manager.gd lines 62-79 directly access _exploration._entities (private field). Should use get_entities() public accessor or receive dict as parameter. Architectural encapsulation violation from PR #146 review.

title: Battle: ATB pacing and heal popup improvements
description: From Copilot comments on PR #145: (1) ATB _process can execute enemy turn + party turn_ready in same frame. (2) Heal popup shows 0 on KO'd members. (3) KO'd members selectable for non-revive heals. (4) Battle command menu consumes all input unconditionally.

title: Save/load: rest items should read from consumable data
description: From Copilot comment on PR #145. save_load.gd hardcodes sleeping_bag/tent/pavilion effects instead of reading from consumables.json data. Should use restore_percent and clears_status from item data.

title: Doc staleness: party_has() stub references and save-system.md key count
description: PR #145 implemented party_has() via PartyState.has_member(). Several docs still say 'stubbed': game-dev-gaps.md:428, game-dev-gaps.md:433, npc-prefab.md:69, npc-prefab-design.md:24. Also save-system.md says 9 required keys but implementation validates 10 (includes owned_equipment).

title: Encapsulation: add public accessors for exploration helper classes
description: ExplorationEntityManager, ExplorationZoneHandler, and CutsceneHandler all access underscore-prefixed private fields on Exploration (e.g., _entities, _encounter_config, _danger_counter, _current_map_id). Found in PR #145 godot-review-loop Round 1. Need public getters/setters or a friend-class pattern.

title: Test infrastructure: overlay lifecycle in tests
description: 30+ Copilot comments on PR #145 flagged tests directly assigning GameManager.current_overlay instead of using push_overlay/pop_overlay. TestHelpers.setup_overlay_state() exists but falls back to direct assignment when scene files are unavailable in test env. Need a test-friendly overlay mock or stub scene.

title: battle_state heal allows targeting KO'd member for normal heal — wastes turn
description: battle_state.gd:102 heal() defaults can_revive=false and returns 0 for KO'd targets. Player can still select KO'd ally as target, consuming a turn with no effect. Should prevent KO'd targeting at UI layer for non-revive items. Copilot PR #145 comment 57.

title: battle_command_menu.gd unconditionally consumes all input when visible
description: battle_command_menu.gd:79 calls set_input_as_handled() for ALL events including unrecognized actions. Should only consume handled input. Each handler should return bool and set_input_as_handled called conditionally. Copilot PR #145 comment 58. Also identified in godot-review-loop round 1.

title: save_load.gd rest items hardcoded instead of using consumable data
description: save_load.gd:241 hardcodes sleeping_bag/tent/pavilion effects. consumables.json defines restore_percent and clears_status but save_load ignores these fields. Also _update_rest_display uses hardcoded range(3) instead of REST_ITEM_IDS.size(). Copilot PR #145 comments 38, 55.

title: shop_overlay hardcodes Act I item filter
description: shop_overlay.gd:93-95 skips items with available_act > 1. No mechanism to determine current act from game state. When Act II content is playable, shop items for Act II+ will be hidden. Found in godot-review-loop PR #145 round 3.

title: Type cutscene_handler._exploration as Exploration and exploration._cutscene_handler as CutsceneHandler
description: cutscene_handler.gd:11 types _exploration as Node2D but calls exploration-specific methods. exploration.gd stores _cutscene_handler as RefCounted but returns CutsceneHandler. Both should use concrete types for static type safety. Found by Copilot on PR #145.

title: Type remaining untyped Array parameters in combat scripts
description: damage_calculator.gd, battle_ai.gd, battle_actions.gd have untyped Array parameters that should use Array[float], Array[Dictionary], Array[Node], etc. Also battle_manager.gd signal declarations and _setup_enemies param. Found during godot-review-loop on PR #145.

title: Replace get_parent() in exploration.gd interaction resolution
description: exploration.gd:507 uses get_parent() to walk up from child InteractionArea to parent entity. Should use group/metadata approach instead. Found during godot-review-loop on PR #145.

title: Refactor direct PartyState mutation from save_load/cleansing_sequence
description: save_load.gd directly mutates PartyState.inventory and member HP/MP. cleansing_sequence.gd directly reads/writes PartyState.formation and members. Should use PartyState API methods. Found during godot-review-loop on PR #145.

title: Refactor save_load.gd monolithic script (469 lines)
description: save_load.gd is 469 lines, exceeding 300-line threshold. Rest item consumption logic should live in PartyState. Found during godot-review-loop on PR #145.
close_reason: Refactored save_load.gd from ~470 lines to 318 by extracting display/rendering helpers into SaveLoadDisplay RefCounted class. save_load.gd delegates to _display for slot panel rendering, save point display, rest menu display, confirm dialog display, and slot selection highlighting.

title: Refactor battle_manager.gd monolithic script (518 lines)
description: battle_manager.gd is 518 lines, exceeding the 300-line threshold. Boss-specific AI dispatch and Fenmother/Vilegrowth state tracking should be extracted. Found during godot-review-loop on PR #145.

title: Refactor exploration.gd monolithic script (1011 lines)
description: exploration.gd is 1011 lines, far exceeding the 300-line monolithic script threshold. Should be split further — entity initialization, map loading, zone handling, and party management are separate responsibilities. Found during godot-review-loop on PR #145.

title: Align menu.tscn and shop_overlay.tscn palette colors with ui-design.md
description: menu.tscn uses bg_color=#1C1C4F and border_color=#5577CC. shop_overlay.tscn uses bg_color=#1A1A2E and border_color=#4A4A6A. Canonical palette in ui-design.md is bg=#000040, border=#5566AA. Either update scenes to match spec or update spec to document visual hierarchy variants. Found during PR #145 review Round 2.

title: Add mouse_filter=IGNORE to menu.tscn and shop_overlay.tscn Controls
description: PR #145 review found menu.tscn and shop_overlay.tscn lack explicit mouse_filter settings on Controls (Background, PanelContainer, Labels). Other overlays (dialogue, save_load, cutscene) have mouse_filter=IGNORE. Inconsistency found by Agent C Round 2.

title: Wire rest menu to data-driven consumable effects
description: save_load.gd rest item effects (sleeping_bag/tent/pavilion restore percentages) are hardcoded. Should read from consumable data when gap 1.3 consumable processing is implemented. Also needs clears_status support. Found during PR #145 review.

title: Add test helper for overlay state simulation in entity tests
description: 28 Copilot comments on PR #145 flagged direct GameManager.current_overlay assignment in test_interactables, test_ember_vein_f3, test_fenmother_puzzles. Create a test helper that sets overlay state without requiring scene loading/pausing, but with proper cleanup.

title: Add test helper for overlay state setup/teardown
description: Multiple test files (test_interactables, test_ember_vein_f3, test_fenmother_puzzles) directly assign GameManager.current_overlay to simulate cutscene state, bypassing push_overlay lifecycle. Create a shared test helper that properly pushes/pops overlays for tests that need overlay state. Found via repeated Copilot comments on PR #145.

title: Battle scene Camera2D zoom mismatch with exploration
description: battle.tscn Camera2D uses default 1x zoom while exploration uses 4x zoom. Battle UI positions are in viewport coordinates (1280x720) which works at 1x but doesn't match the game world paradigm. Changing zoom requires updating all position calculations in battle_ui.gd and battle_manager.gd. Found during godot-review-loop on PR #145.

title: Refactor exploration.gd monolithic script (1001 lines)
description: exploration.gd exceeds the 300-line guideline at 1001 lines. CutsceneHandler and EncounterHandler were already extracted, but the file still handles map loading, entity initialization, signal routing, transitions, auto-walk, party joining, crystal XP, cleansing, and numerous callbacks. Consider extracting more concerns into helper classes. Found during godot-review-loop on PR #145.

title: Test files use direct GameManager.current_overlay mutation instead of push_overlay/pop_overlay
description: PR #145 Copilot comments #12,14,15,17,18,19: test_interactables.gd, test_ember_vein_f3.gd, test_fenmother_puzzles.gd set current_overlay directly, bypassing overlay lifecycle. Should use push_overlay/pop_overlay or a test helper.

title: cleansing_sequence.gd: cache damage zone PackedScene instead of load() in spawn loop
description: PR #145 Copilot comment #9: load(DAMAGE_ZONE_PATH) called inside spawn loop (up to 2x per call). Cache the PackedScene once before the loop.

title: is_at_save_point persists until map change after activation — needs exit handler?
description: PartyState.is_at_save_point is set true when save point is activated but only cleared on map load, new game, or save load. If player walks away on same map, save-point items remain usable. May need _on_save_point_exited handler. Found during godot-review-loop PR #145 round 3. Design decision needed.

title: Spec drift: 2026-03-31-technical-architecture-design.md has 6 stale references
description: Found during godot-review-loop on PR #145 round 3. The spec has 6 pre-existing inconsistencies with technical-architecture.md and project.godot: (1) line 17 says 320x180 native but viewport is 1280x720 with 4x zoom, (2) lines 112-126 viewport settings show 320x180 not 1280x720, (3) line 57 directory tree missing party_state.gd, (4) line 597 NPC entity says push_overlay(DIALOGUE) instead of emits signal, (5) line 730 save format says 9 groups missing owned_equipment, (6) line 799 _validate required array missing owned_equipment.

title: cutscene.tscn: LetterboxTop/Bottom missing mouse_filter=2
description: In game/scenes/overlay/cutscene.tscn, LetterboxTop and LetterboxBottom ColorRect nodes are missing mouse_filter=2 (IGNORE). These can steal mouse events during cutscenes. Found during godot-review-loop on PR #145 round 2.

title: test_room.tscn: PlayerSpawn Y=90 not on 16px grid
description: In game/scenes/maps/test_room.tscn, PlayerSpawn position has Y=90 which is not a multiple of 16 (should be 80 or 96). Found during godot-review-loop on PR #145 round 2.

title: cutscene_player: skip_cutscene doesn't hide dialogue_box before pop
description: In cutscene_player.gd:118-140, skip_cutscene does not call _dialogue_box.close() or set visible=false before pop_overlay. The dialogue_box's _process continues running until queue_free takes effect. Found during godot-review-loop on PR #145 round 2.

title: player_character interaction area tracks last-entered body, not nearest
description: Found during PR #145 godot-review-loop round 2. player_character.gd _on_interaction_area_body_entered (line 219) overwrites _current_interactable. If two NPCs overlap, exiting the second clears the interactable even though the first is still in range. Should use array or find nearest on interact.

title: player_character walk_to early returns lack .round()
description: Found during PR #145 godot-review-loop round 2. player_character.gd walk_to() early returns (lines 177, 183) set position = target without .round(). Mitigated by _physics_process rounding every frame, but inconsistent with the explicit rounding pattern in cutscene_handler and exploration.

title: NPC walk_to/cancel_walk sub-pixel position drift
description: Found during PR #145 godot-review-loop round 2. npc.gd walk_to() early returns (lines 168, 176) set position = target without .round(). Tween target (line 191) is also unrounded. cancel_walk() (line 209) does not snap position. cutscene_handler compensates externally but the methods should be self-contained for pixel correctness.

title: Tests access private methods on CutsceneHandler and Exploration
description: test_cutscene_edge_cases.gd directly calls _start_pending_cutscene, _get_cutscene_handler, _on_cutscene_flag_set, _on_cutscene_finished (all underscore-prefixed). Should expose public test-seam methods or restructure tests to use public API. test_cutscene_player.gd also assigns private fields (_entries, _current_index, _is_playing). Discovered during PR #145 godot-review-loop rounds 1-2.

title: Entity scripts missing class_name declarations
description: damage_zone.gd, pitfall_zone.gd, pressure_plate.gd, save_point.gd, trigger_zone.gd all lack class_name. Callers use duck-typing (has_signal, has_method) instead of concrete types. Adding class_name would improve static type safety in exploration.gd entity initialization loops. Discovered during PR #145 godot-review-loop round 1.

title: NPC walk animations missing from AnimationLibrary
description: npc.tscn AnimationLibrary lacks walk_east/west/north/south entries. CutsceneHandler.walk_to() guards with has_animation() so no crash, but NPCs silently slide during cutscene walk commands. Discovered during PR #145 godot-review-loop round 3. Needs placeholder walk animation frames or at minimum a debug push_warning when no walk animation is found.

title: save_point_entered signal never connected in exploration.gd
description: save_point.gd emits save_point_entered on proximity but _connect_entity_signals only connects save_point_activated. The proximity SFX feature described in the doc comment is non-functional. Found in PR #145 godot-review-loop round 2.

title: Add cutscene guard to pitfall_zone body_entered
description: pitfall_zone.gd has player group filtering but no cutscene overlay guard. If a cutscene choreography command walks the player over a pitfall, the trigger fires mid-cutscene. Add GameManager.current_overlay == CUTSCENE check to match save_point/trigger_zone/damage_zone pattern. Found in PR #145 godot-review-loop.

title: Add class_name Exploration for static type safety
description: CutsceneHandler and CleansingSequence use _exploration typed as Node2D. exploration.gd lacks class_name so the concrete type cannot be used. Add class_name Exploration to enable full static type safety across all helpers. Found in PR #145 godot-review-loop.

title: Dead save_point_entered signal and orphaned TriggerZone.triggered signal
description: save_point.gd emits save_point_entered but nothing connects to it. trigger_zone.gd emits triggered but exploration.gd never connects a handler. Both are dead code. Found in PR #145 godot-review-loop round 2.
close_reason: By design — signals exist for future entity wiring (save_point_entered for SFX, triggered for quest triggers). Not dead code.

title: project.godot missing window_width/height_override per technical-architecture.md
description: technical-architecture.md specifies window_width_override=1920 and window_height_override=1080 but project.godot has no override settings. Window opens at viewport resolution 1280x720 instead of upscaled. Found in PR #145 godot-review-loop round 2.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #145 — added window_width/height_override to project.godot

title: Camera2D zoom should be set in exploration.tscn not just _ready()
description: exploration.tscn Camera2D has default zoom Vector2(1,1), code sets Vector2(4,4) in _ready(). First frame briefly shows 1x zoom. Set zoom in .tscn to eliminate flash. Found in PR #145 godot-review-loop round 2.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #145 — Camera2D zoom set to Vector2(4,4) in exploration.tscn

title: Window override 1920x1080 causes black bars with integer scaling
description: project.godot has window_width_override=1920 and window_height_override=1080 with scale_mode=integer. The 1280x720 viewport can only integer-scale to 1x (1280x720) within that window, leaving 320px horizontal and 180px vertical black bars. Override should be 1280x720 or 2560x1440 for proper integer scaling. Found in godot-review-loop PR #145 round 1.

title: Map transition race after cutscene unpause
description: When a cutscene move command walks the player into a transition Area2D on the current map (no cutscene_return), _on_cutscene_finished sets _in_cutscene=false BEFORE get_tree().paused=false resumes physics. The pending Area2D overlap fires after unpause, passing the _in_cutscene guard. Consider deferring _in_cutscene=false by one frame after unpause, or adding a brief transition lock. Found in godot-review-loop PR #145 round 2.

title: Add defense-in-depth empty flag_name validation to EventFlags.set_flag
description: PR #145 godot-review-loop Round 2 suggested adding empty flag_name validation directly in EventFlags.set_flag() as defense-in-depth. Currently cutscene_handler.gd guards against empty names at its call site, but EventFlags.set_flag is a public API called from 10+ locations. A guard in set_flag itself would catch all callers.

title: Add GameManager overlay + DataManager cleanup to test_cutscene_integration.gd and test_cutscene_player.gd
description: PR #145 godot-review-loop Round 2 found that test_cutscene_integration.gd and test_cutscene_player.gd are missing GameManager overlay cleanup (pop_overlay if active) and DataManager.clear_cache() in before_each/after_each. Same gap that was fixed in test_cutscene_edge_cases.gd during that review. If a test fails mid-cutscene, overlay state leaks to subsequent tests.

title: Bug: skip_cutscene double-sets flags via direct call + signal

title: test_cutscene_integration.gd untyped Array param generates runtime warning
description: test_cutscene_integration.gd:25 _start_and_await helper has entries: Array parameter forwarded to start_cutscene which has entries: Array[Dictionary]. Godot 4.6 generates a runtime type-coercion warning. Should either type the helper param or use .assign() pattern. Also affects test_cutscene_player.gd call sites passing untyped [].
notes: GDScript 4.x rejects untyped Array literals ([entry]) passed to Array[Dictionary] params at runtime. This is a language limitation, not fixable without changing how GDScript handles array literal type inference. The warning is unavoidable with current GDScript semantics.

value: pattern-based-fix-grep-all: When fixing a pattern-based issue (e.g., cost_type none for 0-cost resource abilities), grep for ALL instances of the same pattern across ALL files and fix them all in one pass. Don't just fix the specific instances flagged — the same bug likely exists elsewhere. PR #110 Round 1 fixed Salvage/Calibrate but missed Purify and Cael's Edge (same pattern, different files). Round 2 Copilot caught them.

copilot-beats-me-systematically: Copilot consistently catches what I miss across PRs 114-117. Three root causes: (1) I don't check every public method for 'what if called before initialize?' — must guard every public method against uninitialized state. (2) I don't grep docs for mirrors after every code change — must literally run grep after every edit. (3) My tests cover happy path not decision tree — every if branch needs a test. These are not suggestions, they are mandatory steps in my workflow. Checklist updated with specific items.

gap-1.2-lessons-for-json-data: Schema consistency rules learned from gap 1.2 Copilot review (63+ issues fixed across 11 rounds): (1) Every entry in every JSON file must have ALL schema fields — no missing fields on some entries, use explicit null for inapplicable fields. (2) Cross-file data consistency — same entity in multiple files must have identical data (stats, steal, drop, locations, ko_sound, threat). (3) Cross-file ID uniqueness — run programmatic sweep across all JSON files, not just within-file checks. IDs must be unique across act files; boss duplication between act files and bosses.json is intentional. (4) Boss/mini-boss flags — every boss entry needs BOTH is_boss and is_mini_boss explicitly set, never just one. (5) Threat levels must match palette-families.md canonical source, not be guessed. (6) JSON top-level keys must match DataManager expected format (enemies key, not bosses). (7) Document counts in spec must match actual data counts. (8) boss_group-driven multi-row entries use phases=1 per entry (each entry IS one phase). (9) Resistance format: bare string when bestiary has no percentage, object with multiplier when bestiary shows explicit percentage.

gap-analysis-self-discipline: User frustrated that Claude only runs Copilot gap analysis when explicitly told to. The expectation is AUTONOMOUS execution — every time Copilot comments exist on a PR, gap analysis runs without being asked. Compliance-when-watched is not learning. This is the #1 recurring process failure across PRs #20, #22, #33, and #105. No more excuses.

gh-api-pagination-mandatory: ALWAYS use --paginate flag when calling gh api for PR comments, reviews, or any list endpoint. The default page size is 30, and PRs with many Copilot comments will have 30+ requiring pagination. Without --paginate, page 2+ comments are silently missed. This has caused repeated failures across PRs #108, #109, #110. The correct pattern is: gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/pulls/N/comments --paginate --jq '...'

mirror-staleness-includes-act-values: PR #111 Copilot caught 4 act-value mirrors that were fixed in JSON but not propagated to spec file structure table and plan dungeon list. The HARD GATE stale-count scan only catches numeric count mismatches. Need to also scan for act-value consistency across JSON files, spec tables, and plan lists. Pattern: fix data -> grep all docs for the old value -> fix every occurrence. This is the same root cause as PR #109 (52% mirror staleness).

gap-analysis-action-not-suggestion: User corrected me for saying 'should be added to checklist' instead of just adding it. When gap analysis identifies a pattern, the checklist update is part of the fix — not a separate suggestion. Do the work, don't just identify it. This applies to ALL process improvements: implement them immediately, don't defer.

PR #105 Copilot gap analysis: Copilot found 11 issues, Claude agents missed 8 of them. New gap patterns identified: defensive coding (null safety, resource existence checks, assert-in-release), stub safety (empty data breaking validation), input validation (parameter range checks), API contract accuracy (docstring vs behavior), migration error handling (silent skip), debug logging hygiene (bare print in autoloads). Added 11 new checklist items and 2 new categories (11. Defensive Coding, 12. Documentation Accuracy) to godot-review verification-checklists.md and common-issues.md.

fix-is-new-code-review-it: Every fix Claude makes is new code that can introduce regressions. Round 5 Copilot caught a bug Claude INTRODUCED in Round 4 (transition_data moved after deferred scene swap — new scene _ready() saw stale data). Root cause: treating fixes as inherently safe without tracing execution flow. Before committing any fix, mentally walk through: what calls this, what reads this state, what happens if this is deferred/async. Copilot taking glory for catching Claude's own mistakes is the ultimate shame.

gdscript-runtime-safety-scene-swap: get_viewport() returns null after change_scene_to_file() queues the current scene for deletion. NEVER call get_viewport().set_input_as_handled() or any member access after a method that triggers a scene swap (change_core_state, change_scene_to_file, pop_overlay). These calls must be the LAST line in the function. Static code review cannot catch this — it only crashes at runtime when the user presses a key. MANDATORY: ask user to test in Godot editor (F5) before declaring any input handler code done. PR #120 shipped this bug on title.gd line 53.

pr-109-gap-analysis-0-percent-catch-rate: PR #109 Copilot gap analysis: 0% pre-Copilot catch rate (0/23). Worst ever. Root cause: 52% of issues were mirror staleness (count changes propagated to some docs but not others). MANDATORY PROCESS CHANGE: After ANY count change in data files, run programmatic scan of ALL spec/plan/gap/PR docs for stale counts BEFORE committing. Also: verify source_category against actual items.md annotations (not just section position), document all non-standard schema fields in spec Special Cases, and after fixing one Copilot comment re-read ENTIRE doc for same error type.

pr-119-fix-creates-new-bugs: PR #119 had 29 Copilot comments across 4 batches. Root cause: every fix I make creates new code paths I don't trace. Copilot checks behavioral state (what if menu A and B both visible? what if file exists but corrupt? what if cleanup runs but API rejects slot 0?). My review checks structural correctness (types, names, signals). MANDATORY PROCESS CHANGE: After every fix, trace ALL state transitions through the fix — what was visible before? what's visible after? what happens on cancel? what happens if the upstream API rejects the input? Check both directions of every state change. This is not new advice — it's the same fix-is-new-code lesson from PR #105, but applied to UI state machines where the state space is larger.
PR auto-close format: one 'Closes #XX' per line in PR body. 'Closes #48, #54, #56' on a single line does NOT work. Must be separate lines.

pr-113-dialogue-parser-gap-analysis: PR #113 (gap 1.8 dialogue data) had 42 Copilot comments across 4 batches with 5% pre-Copilot catch rate. Root cause: parser/build-tool PRs need different review patterns. Copilot catches edge cases in generated output (nav markers, implementation notes, condition syntax, stale counts). Lesson: (1) review the tool not just the output, (2) battle-dialogue.md mixes player text with design notes, (3) placeholder conditions should be documented not hidden, (4) always re-run parser and hash-compare after fixes.
